transcript: A nine-year-old kid sobs as he is placed in handcuffs at his elementary school. Cops were called after fourth grader Devin Shepard got into a fight with a classmate during recess. When a teacher intervened, Devin hit the teacher. Do you think your father's or anything, buddy? Do you think mom and dad? No. The boy's father is recording the heartbreaking scene. The footage is a little shaky because he's trembling with anger. De just chill out buddy you're leaving with me promise you nope I'm recording yeah wait I'm worried about my turn to be contacting you I'll get the report tomorrow morning everybody I'll be right there little Devin can't stop crying as he's led out of Needham Elementary School in Franklin, Indiana. Today, Devin's parents, Ronnie and Melissa Shepard, are speaking out. That video, very hard to watch, very difficult to hear your son crying. It was very disturbing. I knew I kept my cool because I was out of school. Devin says the fight on the school playground was triggered after his classmates started bullying him. Devin, who is autistic, was charged with battery and criminal mischief. The local police department says in a statement, officers involved followed both department and juvenile arrest protocol. But Devin's family says they went too far. Any questions or anything, guys? Well, the charges against the child were dropped. The school has now said in a statement that their faculty handled the situation calmly and with compassion.
